place: West Long Branch borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: West Long Branch sits on the Atlantic coast, just a few blocks from the historic Long Branch beach that became a Gilded Age resort in the late 18th century.
The borough’s train station, three blocks from the beach, marks the end of electrified trackage, forcing southbound travelers to switch to diesel.
In 1987, a massive fire destroyed the famed amusement pier and Haunted Mansion in Long Branch, a landmark that once drew crowds to West Long Branch’s waterfront.
West Long Branch shares the same transit village designation approved in 2013, aiming to encourage denser development and revitalization near its train station.
The Seven Presidents Oceanfront Park, named for presidents who visited the resort town, lies just north of West Long Branch and reflects the borough’s historic ties to national leaders.
Garfield’s famous tea house in Elberon was built from railroad ties that once carried his train, a reminder of the borough’s connection to the rail network that serves West Long Branch.
